interactions.summary: The host asks why Jiang chooses good over evil, and Jiang answers with a theory of free choice, insomnia before wrongdoing, demonic entrapment, nonjudgmental divinity, and empathy.

claims.claim: Jiang says the universe contains both radical good and radical evil because free choice is fundamental and gives life its meaningful polarity.
Jiang says he personally chooses good because he cannot sleep if he knows he has brought evil into the world.
Jiang says elite schools such as Yale train people toward a Frankist style of life and that sustained evil traps a person in further evil that must be numbed with drugs or excess.
claims.temporal_scope: Institutional and moral diagnosis stated on 2026-02-15.

claims.claim: Jiang says God does not judge or punish in a crude external sense; people seek good because they themselves want enlightenment and inner peace.

signature_moments.moment: For Jiang, once a person embraces the demonic, that person becomes trapped and must keep feeding the trap to go on living.
signature_moments.source_phrase: once you embrace the demonic, then you are trapped
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the packet's sharpest moral psychology of evil.
